---
title: "Percentile ranks"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Percentile ranks}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(comment = NA)
```

Each player (and key team metrics in `teams-*.json`) carries two percentile flavors:

- **`overall_percentile_<metric>`** — rank of that row’s value within the full Division III sample for the synced season slice.
- **`conference_percentile_<metric>`** — the same idea restricted to pitchers or hitters in the same `conference`.

Percentiles are **0–100** and are computed after derived metrics and baselines are known, so they stay comparable across teams. When conference membership is missing or a conference is tiny, prefer the overall column for stability.
